etymology.description: semantic 攵(攵) (pū, 'to strike; action') + phonetic 交(交) (jiāo) -> result achieved through actions
etymology.interpretation: The character 效 (xiào, 'result') combines the semantic element 攵(攵) (pū, 'to strike; action') with the phonetic element 交(交) (jiāo, 'intersect'). The action element suggests the process or effort involved, while the phonetic 交 suggests interaction or crossing. Together, they imply a situation where actions lead to an intersection or outcome, hence the meaning 'result'.

examples.chinese: 这个药没有明显的效果(這個藥沒有明顯的效果)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège yào méiyǒu míngxiǎn de xiàoguǒ.
examples.english: This medicine doesn't have an obvious effect.

examples.chinese: 实验结果显示出良好的效果(實驗結果顯示出良好的效果)。
examples.pinyin: Shíyàn jiéguǒ xiǎnshì chū liánghǎo de xiàoguǒ.
examples.english: The experiment results show a good effect.

examples.chinese: 努力工作的人通常会看到好的成效(努力工作的人通常會看到好的成效)。
examples.pinyin: Nǔlì gōngzuò de rén tōngcháng huì kàn dào hǎo de chéngxiào.
examples.english: People who work hard usually see good results.
